key_points: Threat: HIGH. Bahrain hosts the US Fifth Fleet headquarters and has experienced missile and drone activity targeting military infrastructure.
Trend: STABLE. Bahrain remains a strategic target due to its military significance.
Air Travel: BAH: OPEN but flights canceled.
Ground Travel: King Fahd Causeway remains operational but heavily congested.

situation_assessment.assessment: HIGH. Bahrain hosts the US Fifth Fleet headquarters and has experienced missile and drone activity targeting military infrastructure.
STABLE. Bahrain remains a strategic target due to its military significance.
Presence of US naval headquarters, proximity to Iranian territory, and strategic maritime importance.
Continued missile interception activity and elevated security posture across military installations.
Continued missile threat against military infrastructure.
Risk remains elevated while US naval operations continue in the region.
King Fahd Causeway remains operational but heavily congested.

airports.name: Bahrain International
airports.notes: Terminal open; security pauses during protest surges

borders.name: King Fahd Causeway (Saudi Arabia)
borders.notes: Limited to force protection convoys; civilian passage intermittent during unrest

domestic_unrest.notes: Shia majority population, historically volatile. No active unrest reported since conflict began.

energy_infra.notes: Refining minimal; reliant on Saudi feedstock via causeway pipeline

evac_routes.label: Manama → Saudi Arabia
evac_routes.notes: Only ground exit from Bahrain. Causeway currently restricted to force protection convoys; civilian passage intermittent. Once in Saudi, follow Saudi ground routes for onward movement.

iw_matrix.notes: BAH: obstructed, adjusted schedules. Regional airspace closures affecting routing.
Sheikh Isa Air Base struck by IRGC drones and missiles Mar 3 04:00Z. NSA Bahrain (5th Fleet HQ) on heightened alert.
Direct IRGC strike on US airbase. Bahrain air defenses activated. Details on damage withheld.
Shia majority population — historically sensitive. No active unrest reported since conflict began.
NSA Bahrain is primary US naval HQ. Heightened security posture. Ordered departure of non-emergency USG personnel.
No direct Bahrain energy strikes confirmed. Regional disruptions (Qatar LNG, Ras Tanura) affect supply chain.
